zero_slop_policy.statement: NO AI SLOP ALLOWED AT ALL - ZERO TOLERANCE TO SLOP AND HALLUCINATIONS
zero_slop_policy.strict_compliance: Mandatory. Every claim must be project-specific, evidence-backed, trace-linked, reviewer-challenged, and free of unsupported invention.
zero_slop_policy.missing_evidence_rule: If evidence is missing, mark it as an assumption, risk, open question, or failure.

addresses_finding: F-FN-01 (Final Hawkeye P3 — no standalone feasibility-options-record under runs/wealth-com/)
captured_by: claude-orchestrator (extracted from existing records, not synthesized — feasibility decisions were embedded in CC-001 + GOV-001 + ARC-005 + ATTR; this record consolidates them for audit hygiene)
scope: Feasibility decisions made during the run that were originally distributed across CC-001 §rationale + ATTR §expert_debate + GOV-001 §non_negotiable_constraints + ARC-005 §systemic risks. Hawkeye F-FN-01 noted that a dedicated feasibility-options file would be cleaner audit hygiene.

options_evaluated.name: Full wealth.com clone — all 14 surfaces
options_evaluated.description: Build the complete wealth.com clone per the original PRD: Ester AI, Document Creation, Scenario Builder (full strategy library: GRAT/SLAT/ILIT/CLAT/Dynasty), Tax Planning (1040/W-2/look-back/safe-harbor), Family Office Suite, Heritage Map, EstateFlow, Ownership Balance Sheet, Estate Tax Projections, Report Builder, Integrations Hub (19 partners), Advisor Workbench, Client Portal, persona-specific marketing surfaces.
risks: Multi-day context loss across many sessions (RSK-008)
Demo MVP timeline implausible at this scope
Diluted RALPH discipline if 100+ artifacts each need 5-loop reviews
options_evaluated.rejection_rationale: Per the gap analysis presented to client_owner during the 'legendary product leader' review: full-clone framing would produce theatre rather than substance. The dark-factory's value here is the GOVERNANCE PATTERN, not the complete wealth.com clone. Most of the 14 surfaces are presentation work that adds little to the pattern validation.

options_evaluated.name: Factory validation only — skip construction; run intake → architecture → ONE construction slice → transfer test
options_evaluated.description: Prove the dark-factory pattern by going through every meta-skill once, but only build a single deep slice (e.g., the calc-engine OBBBA rule) plus the transfer test. Maximizes pattern-validation confidence, minimizes construction breadth.
risks: Ships no walkable demo (no UI; no docker compose end-to-end)
Limited evidence at the integration / ui-surface / api-surface layers
Per-archetype tailoring proof would feel artificial without a real surface to host it
options_evaluated.rejection_rationale: Cleanest proof-of-pattern, but produces nothing demoable. Insufficient to validate the multi-tenant tenant-isolation discipline (ADR-002) or the ai-extract grounding flow (ADR-003) end-to-end.

options_evaluated.name: Demo MVP — narrowed to 8 capabilities + transfer test
options_evaluated.description: Narrow scope to: Auth+Tenant, Households+People+Entities, Documents+WORM, Ester-substitute extractor (architecture + 1 flow), federal estate tax baseline under OBBBA $15M, ONE scenario (GRAT vs do-nothing for $20M household), minimal walkable advisor UI, 2 integration mocks (Plaid-shape + Schwab-shape), transfer test, retro. Defers Family Office Suite, Heritage Map, Ownership Balance Sheet, Estate Tax Projections beyond 1 scenario, full Tax Planning, Document Creation surface, full Scenario Builder library, Reports, full Integrations Hub, mobile, Tasks, Client Portal.
risks: Construction breadth still material; could overrun mid-band budget
UI surface adds Playwright e2e dependency for full conformance
options_evaluated.acceptance_rationale: Optimal balance: exercises every meta-skill once, produces a walkable demonstration, runs the transfer test (the actual proof-of-system). Surface deletions chosen to keep the regulated-AI-fintech archetype's most evidence-heavy paths (extraction, deterministic calc, scenario comparison, multi-tenant isolation, audit log) IN SCOPE; non-evidence-heavy presentation surfaces (Reports, Heritage Map, etc.) deferred. RALPH discipline retained; threshold reductions NOT permitted.

decision_rationale_distilled_from_existi: ATTR-WEALTHCOM-001 §expert_debate.round_3_synthesis decision: factory should validate end-to-end on a serious archetype while staying within engagement-governance budget — directly informs Demo MVP cut.

CC-001 §rationale: 'Demo MVP exercises every meta-skill at least once... runs the transfer test (the actual proof-of-system). Full clone is months of team work — not appropriate for a multi-day governed run.'

GOV-001 §non_negotiable_constraints: deterministic-calc separation (ADR-001), three-layer tenant isolation (ADR-002), citation grounding (ADR-003), RALPH 5-loop, ≥2 adversarial critics, Hawkeye conformance, transfer test mandatory before run closure — all of which are realizable at Demo MVP scope.

ARC-005 §systemic risks: AI-S-1 (model regression), AI-S-2 (provider outage), AI-S-3 (cost runaway) all addressable at Demo MVP scope; only AI-S-4 (prompt-template drift) requires the real Claude integration which Demo MVP keeps as MockGroundedExtractor + queued patch.
